lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Greetings, adventurer. Welcome to the Myths' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the guild.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: The Myths' Guild is home to those who wish to uncover the lost secrets of this world. We investigate the myths of these lands to see if any facts may be hidden within them.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Here, we discover things that others only dream of. However, the cost of entry is not insignificant. Only the most worthy may share in our secrets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Am I worthy?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Not yet. However, that could well change.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: For as long as history remembers, dragons have roamed these lands, but despite how long they've been here, much of their history remains hidden.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: There are tales of course, but tales are not enough for us. We want you to discover the truth. Where did the dragons come from? Why are they here? Who do they serve?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Answer these questions for us. Help us fill our Library of Knowledge. In return, we will grant you access to the guild and you will reap the rewards within.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: So, do you feel up for the challenge?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Where should I begin?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Head to Karamja Wines, Spirits, and Beers at Musa Point. There you'll find Dallas Jones. We've heard that he is investigating the destruction of Crandor. This seems like a good place to start.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. This is too much for me.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: As you wish. If you change your mind, you know where we are.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Absolutely. You've proven your worth to the guild and may enter whenever you please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I gain access to the guild?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Only the most worthy are allowed access into the Myths' Guild. If you wish to enter, you'll need to prove yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: As time passes, that which was once fact, becomes myth. Some tales are just that, tales. However, some tales are something more.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Here at the Myths' Guild, we live to tell the difference between the two. If you wish to join us, you must do the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what myth would you like me to investigate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: How are things progressing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm yet to discover anything.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well return to us once you do. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the origin of Elvarg. I've found some old doors on a ruined island but they're locked. I'm trying to find the key.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well keep at it. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the origin of Elvarg. I've found some old doors on a ruined island but they're locked. I think something important lies behind them.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well keep at it. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was investigating the origin of Elvarg but I accidently released a dragonkin.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well that can't be good. You'd better sort that out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Farewell, adventurer.

lines.text: I'm afraid the Guild is not currently accepting new members. However, we will soon be opening our doors again.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: When we do, you should return here. We may well have a place for you.
